label: Test Quality
summary: Tests that read like placeholders or never prove failure behavior.

rules.label: Happy Path Only Test
rules.description: Tests that assert success expectations without any obvious negative-path signal.
rules.explanation: In Common, Happy Path Only Test matters because the pattern here, Tests that assert success expectations without any obvious negative-path signal, removes a guardrail or signal that reviewers expect before trusting the surrounding code.
rules.fix: For Happy Path Only Test in Common, add any obvious negative-path signal or move the code to a path where that safeguard is guaranteed before the risky work happens.

rules.label: Placeholder Test Body
rules.description: Tests that look skipped, TODO-shaped, or otherwise placeholder-like.
rules.explanation: In Common, Placeholder Test Body matters because the pattern here, Tests that look skipped, TODO-shaped, or otherwise placeholder-like, can keep the suite green while still leaving the risky path unproven.
rules.fix: For Placeholder Test Body in Common, add assertions, negative-path coverage, or explicit failure checks that prove the behavior this pattern currently leaves implicit.

rules.label: Test without Assertion Signal
rules.description: Tests that exercise production code without an obvious assertion or failure signal.
rules.explanation: In Common, Test without Assertion Signal matters because the pattern here, Tests that exercise production code without an obvious assertion or failure signal, removes a guardrail or signal that reviewers expect before trusting the surrounding code.
rules.fix: For Test without Assertion Signal in Common, add an obvious assertion or failure signal or move the code to a path where that safeguard is guaranteed before the risky work happens.
